steps.title: Use the risk relationship
steps.content: Haddad's study links perceived environmental risk to purchase likelihood: when perceived risk increases, likelihood decreases.

steps.title: Apply it to the group
steps.content: Parents of small children had the lowest perceived environmental risk for the skin-care product, so the relationship suggests they might be more likely than the other groups to purchase it. Choices A, B, and C make claims about appeal, equal likelihood, or other priorities that the studies do not support. The answer is D.
